titleStmt.title: Letter from M.C. Seton to the Under Secretary, 1 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: M.C. Seton

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from M.C. Seton to the under secretary for Ireland concerning the ex-military criminal lunatic James Coghlan. According to Seton payment has been passed to the Governor of the Dundrum Criminal Lunatic Asylum for Coghlan's detention. Additionally, Seton, on the behalf of Secretary Chamberlain, requests to know the circumstances of Coghlan's release from the asylum and to which prison he has been sent to serve out the remained of his sentence.At the time this letter was written Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) was serving as under secretary of Ireland. Nathan served as under secretary from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.

correspAction.placeName: India Office, Whitehall, London, S.W., England

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
INDIA OFFICE, WHITEHALL, LONDON, S.W. 1 December, 1915. Sir, With reference to Sir E.O. Farrell's letter
No.19110, dated the 17th ultimo, regarding the exâmilitary
criminal lunatic James Coghlan, I am directed by Mr. Secretary
Chamberlain to state for the information of His Excellency
the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland that the sum of Â£26.12.7.
has been passed for payment to the Governor of the Dundrum
Criminal Lunatic Asylum. Mr. Chamberlain would be glad to be informed of
the circumstances in which Coghlan, who was sentenced to
death for murder in April 1910 and whose sentence was
afterwards commuted by Government to penal servitude for
life, was discharged from the Dundrum Criminal Lunatic
Asylum on the 15th October last, and also to what prison
he has been transferred to serve the remainder of his
sentence. I have the honour to be, Sir,
Your obedient Servant, M.C.Seton The Under Secretary, DUBLIN CASTLE. I Note answer
II Convict Department
For report
E. Aof 2âXIIâ15
